sourceQuestion:
A 1.5 m tall person is standing at a distance of 3 m from a lamp post. The light from the lamp at the top of the post casts her shadow. The length of the shadow is twice her height. What is the height of the lamp post in meters?

A. 1.5
B. 3
C. 4.5
D. 6

parsed.stem: A 1.5 m tall person is standing at a distance of 3 m from a lamp post. The light from the lamp at the top of the post casts her shadow. The length of the shadow is twice her height. What is the height of the lamp post in meters?

sourceQuestion:
A rectangle becomes a square when its length and breadth are reduced by 10 m and 5 m, respectively. During this process, the rectangle loses 650 m^2 of area. What is the area of the original rectangle in square meters?

A. 1125
B. 2250
C. 2924
D. 4000

parsed.stem: A rectangle becomes a square when its length and breadth are reduced by 10 m and 5 m, respectively. During this process, the rectangle loses 650 m^2 of area. What is the area of the original rectangle in square meters?

sourceQuestion:
The perimeters of a circle, a square and an equilateral triangle are equal. Which one of the following statements is true?

A. The circle has the largest area
B. The square has the largest area
C. The equilateral triangle has the largest area
D. All the three shapes have the same area
sourceAnswer: A. The circle has the largest area

parsed.stem: The perimeters of a circle, a square and an equilateral triangle are equal. Which one of the following statements is true?

options.text: The circle has the largest area
The square has the largest area
The equilateral triangle has the largest area
All the three shapes have the same area

sourceQuestion:
A wire would enclose an area of 1936 m^2, if it is bent to a square. The wire is cut into two pieces. The longer piece is thrice as long as the shorter piece. The long and the short pieces are bent into a square and a circle, respectively. Which of the following choices is closest to the sum of the areas enclosed by the two pieces in square meters?

A. 1096
B. 1111
C. 1243
D. 2486

parsed.stem: A wire would enclose an area of 1936 m^2, if it is bent to a square. The wire is cut into two pieces. The longer piece is thrice as long as the shorter piece. The long and the short pieces are bent into a square and a circle, respectively. Which of the following choices is closest to the sum of the areas enclosed by the two pieces in square meters?

sourceQuestion:
On a horizontal ground, the base of a straight ladder is 6 m away from the base of a vertical pole. The ladder makes an angle of 45 degrees to the horizontal. If the ladder is resting at a point located at one-fifth of the height of the pole from the bottom, the height of the pole is ______ meters.

A. 15
B. 25
C. 30
D. 35

parsed.stem: On a horizontal ground, the base of a straight ladder is 6 m away from the base of a vertical pole. The ladder makes an angle of 45 degrees to the horizontal. If the ladder is resting at a point located at one-fifth of the height of the pole from the bottom, the height of the pole is ______ meters.

sourceQuestion:
Suresh wanted to lay a new carpet in his new mansion with an area of 70 x 55 sq. mts. However an area of 500 sq. mts. had to be left out for flower pots. If the cost of carpet is Rs. 50 per sq. mts., how much money (in Rs.) will be spent by Suresh for the carpet now?

A. Rs. 1,65,000
B. Rs. 1,92,500
C. Rs. 2,75,000
D. Rs. 1,27,500

parsed.stem: Suresh wanted to lay a new carpet in his new mansion with an area of 70 x 55 sq. mts. However an area of 500 sq. mts. had to be left out for flower pots. If the cost of carpet is Rs. 50 per sq. mts., how much money (in Rs.) will be spent by Suresh for the carpet now?

sourceQuestion:
A retaining wall with measurements 10 m x 8 m x 6 m was constructed with bricks of dimensions 8 cm x 6 cm x 6 cm. If 60% of the wall consists of bricks, the number of bricks used for the construction is _______ lakhs.

A. 30
B. 80
C. 45
D. 75

parsed.stem: A retaining wall with measurements 10 m x 8 m x 6 m was constructed with bricks of dimensions 8 cm x 6 cm x 6 cm. If 60% of the wall consists of bricks, the number of bricks used for the construction is _______ lakhs.

A. 2
B. 3
C. 5
D. 6

sourceQuestion:
Consider the following inequalities.
I.  2p - q < 8
II. 3q - p >= 12
Which one of the following expressions below satisfies the above two inequalities?
A. p = 0, q = 4
B. p = 2, q = 8
C. 8 < p + q <= 16
D. p + q > 16

parsed.stem:
Consider the following inequalities.
I.  2p - q < 8
II. 3q - p >= 12
Which one of the following expressions below satisfies the above two inequalities?

sourceQuestion:
Which one of the following is a representation (not to scale and in bold) of all values of x satisfying the inequality 2 - 5x >= -(6x - 3)/3 on the real number line?
A. <--- [closed dot] x=1 ------>
B. <--- ------- [open dot] x=1 --->
C. <--- [closed dot at -1] x=-1 ------>
D. <--- ------- [open dot at -1] x=-1 ->
sourceAnswer: C. number line with a closed dot at x = -1 and shading to the left (x <= -1)

parsed.stem: Which one of the following is a representation (not to scale and in bold) of all values of x satisfying the inequality 2 - 5x >= -(6x - 3)/3 on the real number line?

sourceQuestion:
Consider the following inequalities
p^2 - 4q < 4
3p + 2q < 6
where p and q are positive integers.
The value of (p + q) is __________.
A. 2
B. 1
C. 3
D. 4

parsed.stem:
Consider the following inequalities
p^2 - 4q < 4
3p + 2q < 6
where p and q are positive integers.
The value of (p + q) is __________.

sourceQuestion:
Three bells P, Q and R are rung periodically in a school. P is rung every 20 minutes; Q is rung every 30 minutes and R is rung every 50 minutes. If all the three bells are rung at 12:00 PM, when will the three bells ring together again the next time?

A. 5:00 PM
B. 5:30 PM
C. 6:00 PM
D. 6:30 PM

parsed.stem: Three bells P, Q and R are rung periodically in a school. P is rung every 20 minutes; Q is rung every 30 minutes and R is rung every 50 minutes. If all the three bells are rung at 12:00 PM, when will the three bells ring together again the next time?

sourceQuestion:
The following line graph shows f(x). Identify the correct expression for f(x) from the options below.

A. f(x) = -1 - |x - 1|
B. f(x) = -1 + |x - 1|
C. f(x) = -2 + |x - 1|
D. f(x) = -2 - |x - 1|

parsed.stem: The following line graph shows f(x). Identify the correct expression for f(x) from the options below.

sourceQuestion:
The plot below shows the relationship between the mortality risk of cardiovascular disease and the number of steps a person walks per day. Based on the data, which one of the following options is true?

A. The risk reduction on increasing the steps/day from 0 to 10000 is less than the risk reduction on increasing the steps/day from 10000 to 20000.
B. The risk reduction on increasing the steps/day from 0 to 2000 is less than the risk reduction on increasing the steps/day from 10000 to 20000.
C. For any 5000 increment in steps/day the largest risk reduction occurs on going from 0 to 5000.
D. For any 5000 increment in steps/day the largest risk reduction occurs on going from 15000 to 20000.
sourceAnswer: C. For any 5000 increment in steps/day the largest risk reduction occurs on going from 0 to 5000.

parsed.stem: The plot below shows the relationship between the mortality risk of cardiovascular disease and the number of steps a person walks per day. Based on the data, which one of the following options is true?

options.text: The risk reduction on increasing the steps/day from 0 to 10000 is less than the risk reduction on increasing the steps/day from 10000 to 20000.
The risk reduction on increasing the steps/day from 0 to 2000 is less than the risk reduction on increasing the steps/day from 10000 to 20000.
For any 5000 increment in steps/day the largest risk reduction occurs on going from 0 to 5000.
For any 5000 increment in steps/day the largest risk reduction occurs on going from 15000 to 20000.

sourceQuestion:
The fuel consumed by a motorcycle during a journey while travelling at various speeds is indicated in the graph below (fuel consumption in litres per kilometre vs speed in km/h). The distances covered during four laps of the journey are listed in the table below:

Lap | Distance (kilometres) | Average Speed (kilometres per hour)
P   | 40                    | 15
Q   | 75                    | 50
R   | 40                    | 75
S   | 20                    | 10

From the given data, we can conclude that the fuel consumed per kilometre was least during the lap

A. P  B. Q  C. R  D. S

sourceQuestion:
The monthly rainfall chart based on 50 years of rainfall in Agra is shown in the following figure. Which of the following are true?

i. On average, it rains more in July than in December.
ii. Every year, the amount of rainfall in August is more than that in January.
iii. July rainfall can be estimated with better confidence than February rainfall.
iv. In August, there is at least 100 mm of rainfall.

A. (i) and (ii)  B. (i) and (iii)  C. (ii) and (iv)  D. (iii) and (iv)

parsed.stem:
The monthly rainfall chart based on 50 years of rainfall in Agra is shown in the following figure. Which of the following are true?

i. On average, it rains more in July than in December.
ii. Every year, the amount of rainfall in August is more than that in January.
iii. July rainfall can be estimated with better confidence than February rainfall.
iv. In August, there is at least 100 mm of rainfall.

sourceQuestion:
Two finance companies, P and Q, declared fixed annual rates of interest on the amounts invested with them. The rates of interest offered by these companies may differ from year to year. Year-wise annual rates of interest offered by these companies are shown by the line graph below. If the amounts invested in the companies P and Q in 2006 are in the ratio 8 : 9, then the amounts received after one year as interests from P and Q would be in the ratio:

A. 2 : 3
B. 3 : 4
C. 6 : 7
D. 4 : 3

sourceQuestion:
The growth of bacteria (lactobacillus) in milk leads to curd formation. A minimum bacterial population density of 0.8 (in suitable units) is needed to form curd. In the graph below, the population density of lactobacillus in 1 litre of milk is plotted as a function of time, at two different temperatures, 25 degrees C and 37 degrees C.

Consider the following statements based on the data shown above:

i. The growth in bacterial population stops earlier at 37 degrees C as compared to 25 degrees C.
ii. The time taken for curd formation at 25 degrees C is twice the time taken at 37 degrees C.

Which one of the following options is correct?

A. Only i
B. Only ii
C. Both i and ii
D. Neither i nor ii

parsed.stem:
The growth of bacteria (lactobacillus) in milk leads to curd formation. A minimum bacterial population density of 0.8 (in suitable units) is needed to form curd. In the graph below, the population density of lactobacillus in 1 litre of milk is plotted as a function of time, at two different temperatures, 25 degrees C and 37 degrees C.

Consider the following statements based on the data shown above:

i. The growth in bacterial population stops earlier at 37 degrees C as compared to 25 degrees C.
ii. The time taken for curd formation at 25 degrees C is twice the time taken at 37 degrees C.

Which one of the following options is correct?

sourceQuestion: Let G_1 = (V, E_1) and G_2 = (V, E_2) be connected graphs on the same vertex set V with more than two vertices. If G_1 intersect G_2 = (V, E_1 intersect E_2) is not a connected graph, then the graph G_1 union G_2 = (V, E_1 union E_2) A. cannot have a cut vertex  B. must have a cycle  C. must have a cut-edge (bridge)  D. has chromatic number strictly greater than those of G_1 and G_2

options.text: has chromatic number strictly greater than those of G_1 and G_2

sourceQuestion: Which of the following graphs has an Eulerian circuit? A. Any k-regular graph where k is an even number.  B. A complete graph on 90 vertices.  C. The complement of a cycle on 25 vertices.  D. None of the above
sourceAnswer: C. The complement of a cycle on 25 vertices

parsed.stem: Which of the following graphs has an Eulerian circuit?

options.text: Any k-regular graph where k is an even number.
The complement of a cycle on 25 vertices.

sourceQuestion:
In the graph below, the concentration of a particular pollutant in a lake is plotted over (alternate) days of a month in winter (average temperature 10 degrees C) and a month in summer (average temperature 30 degrees C).

Consider the following statements based on the data shown above:
i. Over the given months, the difference between the maximum and the minimum pollutant concentrations is the same in both winter and summer.
ii. There are at least four days in summer month such that the pollutant concentrations on those days are within 1 ppm of the pollutant concentrations on the corresponding days in the winter month.

Which one of the following options is correct?

A. Only i
B. Only ii
C. Both i and ii
D. Neither i nor ii

parsed.stem:
In the graph below, the concentration of a particular pollutant in a lake is plotted over (alternate) days of a month in winter (average temperature 10 degrees C) and a month in summer (average temperature 30 degrees C).

Consider the following statements based on the data shown above:
i. Over the given months, the difference between the maximum and the minimum pollutant concentrations is the same in both winter and summer.
ii. There are at least four days in summer month such that the pollutant concentrations on those days are within 1 ppm of the pollutant concentrations on the corresponding days in the winter month.

Which one of the following options is correct?

sourceQuestion:
Which of the following function(s) is an accurate description of the graph for the range(s) indicated?

i.   y = 2x + 4    for  -3 <= x <= -1
ii.  y = |x| - 1   for  -1 <= x <= 2
iii. y = |x + 1|   for  -1 <= x <= 2
iv.  y = (1/2)x - 1   for   2 <= x <= 3

A. i, ii and iii only
B. i, ii and iv only
C. i and iv only
D. ii and iv only
sourceAnswer: B. i, ii and iv only

parsed.stem:
Which of the following function(s) is an accurate description of the graph for the range(s) indicated?

i.   y = 2x + 4    for  -3 <= x <= -1
ii.  y = |x| - 1   for  -1 <= x <= 2
iii. y = |x + 1|   for  -1 <= x <= 2
iv.  y = (1/2)x - 1   for   2 <= x <= 3

sourceQuestion:
The annual average rainfall in a tropical city is 200 mm. On a particular rainy day (24-hour period), the cumulative rainfall experienced by the city is shown in the graph below. Over the 24-hour period, 50% of the rainfall falling on a rooftop, which had an obstruction-free area of 50 m^2, was harvested into a tank. What is the total volume of water collected in the tank (in litres)?

A. 25,000  B. 18,750  C. 7,500  D. 3,125

parsed.stem: The annual average rainfall in a tropical city is 200 mm. On a particular rainy day (24-hour period), the cumulative rainfall experienced by the city is shown in the graph below. Over the 24-hour period, 50% of the rainfall falling on a rooftop, which had an obstruction-free area of 50 m^2, was harvested into a tank. What is the total volume of water collected in the tank (in litres)?

sourceQuestion:
In a detailed study of annual crow births in India, it was found that there was relatively no growth during the period 2002 to 2004 and a sudden spike from 2004 to 2006. In another unrelated study, it was found that the revenue from cracker sales in India which remained fairly flat from 2002 to 2004, saw a sudden spike in 2006 before declining again in 2008. The solid line in the graph below refers to annual births of crows and the dashed line refers to the annual revenue from cracker sales. Choose the most appropriate inference from the above data.

A. There is a strong correlation between crow birth and cracker sales.
B. Cracker usage increases crow birth rate.
C. Increase in cracker sales is causing an increase in the crow population.
D. Increased birth rate of crows will increase the sale of crackers.
sourceAnswer: C. Increase in cracker sales is causing an increase in the crow population.

parsed.stem: In a detailed study of annual crow births in India, it was found that there was relatively no growth during the period 2002 to 2004 and a sudden spike from 2004 to 2006. In another unrelated study, it was found that the revenue from cracker sales in India which remained fairly flat from 2002 to 2004, saw a sudden spike in 2006 before declining again in 2008. The solid line in the graph below refers to annual births of crows and the dashed line refers to the annual revenue from cracker sales. Choose the most appropriate inference from the above data.

options.text: There is a strong correlation between crow birth and cracker sales.
Cracker usage increases crow birth rate.
Increase in cracker sales is causing an increase in the crow population.
Increased birth rate of crows will increase the sale of crackers.

sourceQuestion:
Consider the following equations of straight lines:
Line L1 : 2x - 3y = 5
Line L2 : 3x + 2y = 8
Line L3 : 4x - 6y = 5
Line L4 : 6x - 9y = 6

Which one among the following is the correct statement?

A. L1 is parallel to L2 and L1 is perpendicular to L2.
B. L2 is parallel to L4 and L2 is perpendicular to L1.
C. L2 is perpendicular to L4 and L1 is parallel to L3.
D. L4 is perpendicular to L2 and L4 is parallel to L3.
sourceAnswer: D. L4 is perpendicular to L2 and L4 is parallel to L3.

parsed.stem:
Consider the following equations of straight lines:
Line L1 : 2x - 3y = 5
Line L2 : 3x + 2y = 8
Line L3 : 4x - 6y = 5
Line L4 : 6x - 9y = 6

Which one among the following is the correct statement?

options.text: L1 is parallel to L2 and L1 is perpendicular to L2.
L2 is parallel to L4 and L2 is perpendicular to L1.
L2 is perpendicular to L4 and L1 is parallel to L3.
L4 is perpendicular to L2 and L4 is parallel to L3.

sourceQuestion:
If log(P) = (1/2)log(Q) = (1/3)log(R), then which of the following options is TRUE?

A. P^2 = Q^(1/2)R^2
B. Q^2 = PR
C. Q^2 = R^2P^2
D. R = P^(1/2)Q^2

parsed.stem: If log(P) = (1/2)log(Q) = (1/3)log(R), then which of the following options is TRUE?

sourceQuestion:
If p >= 0 and p^q = p^(r-1) = q^r, what is the value of the product p*q*r?

A. -1
B. 1/p^r
C. 1
D. p*q^r

parsed.stem: If p >= 0 and p^q = p^(r-1) = q^r, what is the value of the product p*q*r?
